strengths: dbt and BigQuery experience with modeling
Demonstrated data quality and CI/CD practices
weaknesses: No explicit MWAA or Prefect experience
Backend service deployment experience less explicit
improvement_bullets: Developed robust Python backend services and REST APIs to facilitate data exchange between internal platforms and 3P vendors, ensuring reliable data ingestion and retrieval.
Implemented comprehensive monitoring and alerting frameworks for production ELT pipelines using Great Expectations and custom Airflow sensors to proactively resolve data quality issues.
Managed cloud infrastructure security and resource access by configuring IAM policies and KMS encryption for secure data storage in GCS and AWS S3 environments.
Deployed containerized Python applications on ECS/EKS, utilizing CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions to automate testing, deployment, and infrastructure scaling.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams in a regulated data environment, ensuring strict adherence to data governance standards and security protocols while scaling operational infrastructure.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer opportunity with SafeLease. I am interested in this opportunity because SafeLease’s mission to modernize P&C insurance by controlling the full stack—from product design to the underlying data infrastructure—perfectly aligns with my passion for building high-scale, reliable data systems. What interests me about working for SafeLease is its unique position as a profitable, high-growth insurtech company that values technical rigor alongside business agility. I believe my expertise in architecting end-to-end ELT pipelines and my proficiency in Python-based backend development would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on both technical scalability and business-critical reliability. In fact, at my recent project, I designed a production-grade ELT pipeline that ingested complex economic data from the FRED API into BigQuery. By implementing automated data quality checks with Great Expectations and orchestrating the flow with Airflow, I reduced manual data reconciliation efforts and ensured that downstream stakeholders had access to a clean, trustworthy dataset for strategic decision-making. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer role at SafeLease would allow me to bring my data modeling expertise to your goal of creating a unified, reliable view of the business. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my cross-system integration skills. While there, I collaborated with engineers to extract and transform data from REST APIs and relational databases, effectively synchronizing data across systems to improve accuracy by 60% and streamline UI feature development. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in backend Python services in working on the data platform infrastructure at SafeLease. I know that my experience with robust data pipelines and my focus on automation align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
